consistencyPolicy.authority: Every authoritative mutation commits only inside the provider context that owns the changed data.
consistencyPolicy.localAtomicity: A local atomic unit includes one provider context and its owned tables only. All statements in the unit commit or roll back together.
consistencyPolicy.distributedTransaction: Forbidden. No shared SQLite transaction, two-phase commit, cross-context Unit of Work, or transaction object may cross a public port.
consistencyPolicy.successAcknowledgement: A target mutation may acknowledge success only after its authoritative local unit commits, every required synchronous outcome is known, and the approved Audit delivery requirement is satisfied.
consistencyPolicy.unknownOutcome: Timeout, disconnect, or process loss after dispatch but before a conclusive result is an unknown outcome, never assumed success or failure.
consistencyPolicy.retry: No Workforce HTTP command or coordinator/provider mutation is automatically retried. correlationId is diagnostic and is not an idempotency key.
consistencyPolicy.compensation: Only local database rollback before commit is generally approved. No cross-context compensating write is claimed safe without an explicit provider contract, concurrency token, and test.
consistencyPolicy.reconciliation: Unknown/partial outcomes are operator-visible consistency incidents. Reconciliation may inspect provider-owned facts through public queries only; it may not repair with foreign SQL.
consistencyPolicy.audit: Audit is mandatory for every mutation. Exact durable acceptance/event versus synchronous append is decided by WF-PRE-12; until then every target mutation route remains disabled.
consistencyPolicy.routeAdmission: A variant is target-admissible only when its routeAdmission is target_read_admissible_now or all named release gates have passed. This decision alone authorizes no routing.
consistencyPolicy.legacyHold: Frozen legacy cross-context transactions remain authoritative only for explicitly held variants. They are debt containment, not a target exception.
consistencyPolicy.schema: No schema, authority, receipt table, workflow journal, outbox, or migration-router change is approved by WF-PRE-11.

outcomeStates.meaning: No authoritative mutation was dispatched; safe to return the known error.
The single-context atomic unit failed and all its writes rolled back; safe to return the mapped error.
The authoritative local unit committed and all required acknowledgement conditions are satisfied.
A provider commit is known but Audit/response acknowledgement failed; return technical failure, never automatically replay, and reconcile.
Dispatch occurred but commit outcome cannot be proven; return technical failure, quarantine automatic replay, and reconcile.

legacyEvidence.atomicity: One SQLite transaction writes teachers and optionally users, user_roles, and user_branch_access; Audit append occurs after commit outside that transaction.
legacyEvidence.rollback: Teacher and Identity writes roll back together on repository failure; Audit failure cannot roll back the committed business transaction.

legacyEvidence.atomicity: One SQLite transaction writes teachers and Identity users/roles/branch grants/sessions as applicable; Audit append occurs after commit.
legacyEvidence.rollback: Teacher and Identity writes roll back together on repository failure; Audit remains outside.

legacyEvidence.atomicity: Blocker reads occur before one SQLite transaction updates Teacher/User and deletes sessions; Audit append occurs after commit.
legacyEvidence.rollback: Teacher/User/session writes roll back together; blocker-check race and Audit are not in the transaction.

legacyEvidence.atomicity: Password update and session deletion are separate autocommit statements; Audit follows both.
legacyEvidence.rollback: A failure after password update can leave sessions valid and cannot restore the prior credential.

legacyEvidence.atomicity: Each Workforce row mutation autocommits; Audit follows as a separate call.
legacyEvidence.rollback: Audit failure cannot roll back the Teacher/Working Hour mutation.

atomicUnits.name: Teacher aggregate mutation
atomicUnits.isolation: SQLite write transaction in the future owned adapter
atomicUnits.commitRule: Exactly one tenant-scoped Teacher mutation commits or the unit rolls back

atomicUnits.name: Teacher Working Hour mutation
atomicUnits.isolation: SQLite write transaction in the future owned adapter
atomicUnits.commitRule: The adapter must recheck overlap and insert while protected from a same-Teacher/same-weekday competing insert inside the owned transaction

atomicUnits.name: Teacher portal lifecycle mutation
atomicUnits.commitRule: The requested Identity lifecycle outcome and required session invalidation commit together or roll back together

atomicUnits.name: Credential reset and session invalidation
atomicUnits.commitRule: Credential replacement and invalidation of every session for the tenant-scoped Teacher User commit together or roll back together

atomicUnits.name: Audit record append
atomicUnits.commitRule: One immutable minimum audit intent is accepted exactly according to the WF-PRE-12 delivery decision

branchResolution.decision: A later Branch lifecycle change does not roll back a committed Workforce reference.

archiveBlockers.consistency: two provider request-time snapshots before Teacher archive
archiveBlockers.decision: No distributed lock exists across Academic Groups, Lesson Delivery, and Workforce. A concurrent assignment/Lesson can race the archive decision.
archiveBlockers.routeEffect: all archive variants remain legacy hold until a provider reservation/version protocol or an approved reconciliation invariant closes the race

teacherReference.decision: Reset preserves current behavior and requires existence/configured portal access, not active Teacher status.
teacherReference.routeEffect: accepted for Identity-local reset after Audit delivery is decided

variantConsistencyMatrix.failure: Any required provider unknown/unavailable outcome fails the whole query; no partial DTO
Any required provider unknown/unavailable outcome fails the whole query; no partial profile
Before commit rolls back; after commit but before acknowledgement is committed_unacknowledged
Legacy shared transaction rolls back Teacher and Identity together; Audit remains outside
releaseGates: explicit future consistency revision with reservation/idempotency semantics

variantConsistencyMatrix.failure: Before commit rolls back; after commit but before acknowledgement is committed_unacknowledged
Legacy shared transaction rolls back Teacher and Identity together; Audit remains outside
releaseGates: explicit future consistency revision with concurrency/idempotency semantics

variantConsistencyMatrix.failure: Provider blocker snapshots cannot be made atomic with Teacher status without a reservation/version protocol
releaseGates: explicit provider reservation/version or approved reconciliation invariant

variantConsistencyMatrix.failure: Both blocker TOCTOU and Workforce/Identity split-commit risk remain
releaseGates: explicit provider reservation/version and Identity consistency revision

variantConsistencyMatrix.failure: Before commit rolls back; after commit but before acknowledgement is committed_unacknowledged

ordering: Atomically replace credential and invalidate all sessions
variantConsistencyMatrix.failure: Identity failure rolls back credential and sessions together; after Identity commit but before acknowledgement is committed_unacknowledged

ordering: Read Working Hours and Teacher names
variantConsistencyMatrix.failure: Owned read failure fails the query
Overlap/insertion failure rolls back; after commit but before acknowledgement is committed_unacknowledged
Delete failure rolls back; after commit but before acknowledgement is committed_unacknowledged

routeAdmissionSummary.authorizationEffect: None. WF-PRE-11 is a design gate and does not authorize even read routing before WF-PRE-16.

retryAndIdempotency.queryRetry: Only the future caller may retry a safe query within its own deadline; no retry is promised by the public contract.
retryAndIdempotency.knownFailureReplay: A rolled_back/not_started command may be manually reissued as a new command after diagnosis.
retryAndIdempotency.unknownFailureReplay: Forbidden until reconciliation proves the provider outcome.
retryAndIdempotency.stateSettingCommands: Semantic convergence does not make transport replay safe; Audit and response duplication still require proof.

reconciliationPolicy.owner: Operations with the authoritative provider owner
allowedActions: Read Workforce or provider state through approved public queries
Append a missing Audit intent only after authoritative state is proven
Classify the incident and choose an explicitly approved provider command
forbiddenActions: Blind replay of a command with unknown outcome
Fabricating success to the original request
Deleting or rewriting immutable Audit history
Cross-context rollback without a versioned compensation contract
reconciliationPolicy.runbookGate: WF-PRE-14 must provide exact commands, evidence capture, owners, thresholds, and escalation.

consistencyRisks.issue: Portal-enabled create/update and portal archive currently depend on a shared cross-context SQLite transaction.
consistencyRisks.treatment: Keep variants on frozen legacy; no target compensation is invented.

consistencyRisks.issue: Mandatory Audit cannot roll back a previously committed provider transaction.
consistencyRisks.treatment: All target writes remain disabled until WF-PRE-12 decides delivery/acceptance and WF-PRE-13 tests failure states.

consistencyRisks.issue: Group/Lesson archive blocker snapshots can race a Teacher status change.
consistencyRisks.treatment: All archive variants remain legacy hold until reservation/version or a governed reconciliation invariant exists.

consistencyRisks.issue: No external idempotency key or durable workflow receipt exists, so ambiguous command replay can duplicate or overwrite effects.
consistencyRisks.treatment: No automatic retry; unknown outcomes require reconciliation.

consistencyRisks.issue: Legacy password reset can commit the credential before session deletion fails.
consistencyRisks.treatment: Target reset requires ID-ATOMIC-02; legacy remains until the Identity adapter contract proves atomicity.

guards.rule: No target atomic unit may contain tables owned by more than one bounded context.
No target write route is enabled by WF-PRE-11.
A variant marked legacy_hold cannot call target mutation ports.
Unknown provider outcomes cannot be automatically retried or translated to success.
Credential reset cannot acknowledge success unless credential replacement and all-session invalidation are one Identity atomic unit.
Archive cannot route target while blocker TOCTOU has no approved close/reconciliation protocol.
No direct foreign SQL, cross-context Unit of Work, distributed transaction, workflow journal, outbox, or event is introduced by this decision.

deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-12: Audit delivery/acceptance and all other integration-event requirements; may supersede synchronous WF-SEAM-07 only with exact reliability semantics
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-13: Executable local/provider atomicity, timeout, rollback, unknown-outcome, tenant, blocker-race, Audit, and parity tests
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-14: Exact route variants, cohort, stop/rollback thresholds, incident reconciliation commands, evidence, and owners
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-16: Final migration authorization after every mandatory criterion passes
